title: Finding the real distance from a map measurement
problem: A map has a scale of $1:50\,000$. A road measures $6$ cm on the map. Find the real length of the road in kilometres.

steps.working: A scale of $1:50\,000$ means $1$ cm on the map represents $50\,000$ cm in real life.

steps.instruction: Multiply the map distance by the scale factor.

answer: The real length of the road is $3$ km.

title: Finding the map distance from a real distance
problem: A map has a scale of $1:25\,000$. The real distance between two lakes is $5$ km. Find the distance between the lakes on the map in centimetres.

steps.instruction: Convert the real distance to centimetres.
Divide by the scale factor to find the map distance.

answer: The distance on the map is $20$ cm.

title: Finding the real area from a scale drawing
problem: A plan has a scale of $1:500$. A rectangular garden on the plan measures $4$ cm by $6$ cm. Find the real area of the garden in square metres.

steps.instruction: Find the real length of each side in centimetres.
Alternative method: use the area scale factor.
steps.working: Area scale factor $= 500^2 = 250\,000$ \\ Plan area $= 4 \times 6 = 24$ cm² \\ Real area $= 24 \times 250\,000 = 6\,000\,000$ cm² $= 600$ m²

answer: The real area of the garden is $600$ m².
